cross_scene_arcs.title: Bess and Raleigh's Secret Romance
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Bess's relationship with Raleigh progresses from shared smiles and conversations to intimate confession, pregnancy, and a secret marriage, culminating in public disgrace when discovered by Elizabeth.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Bess and Raleigh exchange a smile, showing mutual interest.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Secretly married to Raleigh, but beaten by Elizabeth and facing his arrest.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The stability of their marriage and Raleigh's future prospects after arrest.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Bess's Familial Loyalty Conflict
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Bess refuses to aid her Papist cousin George Throckmorton, leading to his execution for treason; she later confesses her guilt to Raleigh, revealing the emotional toll of her loyalty to the Queen.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Confronted by George, Bess refuses help, citing loyalty to Elizabeth and fear of danger.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Guilt-ridden after her cousin's death, she confesses to Raleigh, deepening their intimacy.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Lingering guilt and potential repercussions from her family's fate.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Bess's Court Status and Royal Favor
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Bess begins as a favored maid expressing marital desires and gaining Elizabeth's trust, but her secret marriage leads to a fall from grace; she is later partially forgiven after bearing a child.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Favored maid of honour who states her personal criteria for marriage to Elizabeth.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Forgiven by Elizabeth with a touch on the cheek after her child is blessed.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether her status at court is fully restored or remains precarious.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Bess's Personal Aspirations vs. Reality
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Bess aspires to marry for love but, faced with pregnancy and legal constraints, opts for a secret marriage to Raleigh, highlighting the conflict between personal desires and courtly obligations.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Desires to marry an honest, attractive man based on her own choice.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Secretly married to Raleigh, planning to hide her pregnancy and leave court.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether she can achieve a stable, open family life without royal disapproval.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Mary Stuart's Fate and Moral Reckoning
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Elizabeth initially defends Mary Stuart as a cousin and guest, later refuses to sanction her execution by asserting royal authority, but ultimately realizes she was manipulated into the execution, leading to moral dread and preparation for war.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Protective stance towards Mary Stuart, resisting advisors' pressure.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Burdened by moral reckoning and steeling herself for the coming battle.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The moral consequences of executing an anointed queen and the justification for Philip's holy war.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Rejection of Marriage and Personal Bonds
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Elizabeth rejects marriage proposals from Philip and the Archduke, citing her dedication to England, while forming a close bond with Raleigh, whom she knights and appoints to her guard, revealing her need for admiration and fear of abandonment.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Facing resentment from Philip over rejected marriage proposal.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Raleigh knighted and appointed to her guard, but her plea for him to stay unaddressed.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Raleigh's desire to sail to the New World versus Elizabeth's request for him to remain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Confronting the Spanish Armada
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Elizabeth assesses defense plans amid foreign threats, leads a strategy session to prevent the Spanish fleet from reaching Calais, and finally addresses her army in armor, assuming command as their general.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Receiving briefings on threats and evaluating defenses.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Armored and ready to lead the army against invasion.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The impending battle with Spanish forces.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Aging and Assassination Attempt
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Elizabeth privately confronts her aging reflection, showing vulnerability with Bess, and later survives an assassination attempt unharmed, remaining composed.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Unharmed but frozen after the attack.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The broader conspiracy threat and her mortality.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Rise to Royal Favor
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Raleigh progresses from a failed public gesture to gaining Elizabeth's attention and favor through strategic presentations and candid exchanges, culminating in his knighthood and appointment to her guard.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Unrecognized courtier seeking the Queen's notice
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Knighted as Sir Walter Raleigh and bound to court service as Captain of the Guard
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His desire to sail to the New World remains unfulfilled

cross_scene_arcs.title: Secret Marriage and Royal Wrath
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Raleigh's secret marriage to Bess provokes Elizabeth's anger, leading to his arrest after he defends his wife, but eventually results in forgiveness following the birth of his son.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Arrested and then forgiven by the Queen, with his family acknowledged

cross_scene_arcs.title: Naval Ambitions and Colonial Dreams
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Raleigh petitions for a colonial warrant in Virginia, is diverted to court duties, but later returns to naval service and executes a daring fire ship attack against the Spanish fleet.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Petitioner requesting royal warrant for colonization
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Active participant in naval warfare, with colonial ambitions sidelined
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The grant of the colonial warrant remains uncertain
